name: Browser/mobile camera service
summary: Host a small camera-capture service on the LAN so a phone, tablet or any browser becomes a camera for this node. Serves a mobile getUserMedia page (rear camera) and a same-origin /ingest endpoint that forwards every captured frame to urirun-connector-camera's pipeline (OCR, barcodes/QR, inspection with alerts, scene description). No app install, no CORS.
description: webcam:// turns 'add a camera over the network' into a URI. webcam://host/server/command/start launches an HTTP server (stdlib) bound to the LAN and returns the URL to open on a phone — the page uses the browser camera to capture frames and POSTs them to /ingest, which runs the configured `action` (analyze | inspect | barcodes | receipt | ocr | describe) via urirun-connector-camera. webcam://host/server/query/status reports the URL, health and capture count; webcam://host/server/command/stop stops it; webcam://host/captures/query/list returns recent frames with their OCR/barcode/inspection results. An optional shared `token` gates access. Ideal for mobile scanning into the same office flow used by usb:// + camera:// + ocr://.

system: browser with getUserMedia (served over HTTPS or localhost for camera access)
